note:
Play商店的版本自10月以来就没有更新过。例如，根据Sensor Tower的数据，2020年30月30日。
 根据Android Police的报告，该应用已挂起并且无法加载内容，并且报告了连接问题。我们了解到问题出在他们所描述的问题上-当用户下载该应用程序时，该应用程序要么会挂在带有“ T”标志旋转的初始加载屏幕上，要么会在启动时立即报告服务器错误。无论哪种情况，它都不会加载任何应用程序体验。
  Play商店上最近的用户评论指出了这些问题，例如“将无法打开”，“该应用甚至无法正常工作”，“绝对糟糕甚至无法正常工作”，“不会一直保持检查连接状态， “ 和更多。一位用户甚至要求开发人员对众多投诉做出回应，说：“请回复人们的评论。它没有加载。”另一个暗示问题是Google的过错，指出“在Google取消之前，它工作得很好。”
 不过，谷歌没有取消它。在Google采取此行动之前，Trump 2020 Android应用程序实际上已经遇到了一段时间。
 例如，大约一个月前的一条推文描述了一系列类似的问题：
  谷歌通过TechCrunch告知该应用尚未被Play商店禁止，只是因为其无法正常运行而被暂停。如果问题得以解决，可以将其恢复。该公司还表示，在撤消该应用之前，曾尝试与该应用的开发者联系，但从未收到回应。
 谷歌发言人表示：“特朗普2020竞选版应用程序最近停止工作，我们多次与开发人员联系，试图让他们解决该问题。” “人们希望从Google Play下载的应用程序提供最低限度的功能，并且我们的政策是从商店中删除不可用的应用程序（如果未修复）。” 
尽管Android出现问题，我们发现iOS版本仍然可以在首次启动时加载，并且可以在注册时将确认代码发送到电话号码。但是，当您访问应用程序的主屏幕时，它现在还会显示一条错误消息。但是，此错误不会影响您浏览iOS上过去的内容的能力。
  根据Sensor Tower的数据，自2021年2月7日以来，Android版本尚未发现任何新安装。该公司还指出，Trump 2020 Android应用程序安装了约84万次，而iOS上接近150万次。
 这不是Trump 2020应用程序的问题首次成为头条新闻。
 在去年美国总统大选前的几个月中，许多TikTok用户决定在其用户评论中拖曳该应用。 （由于某些原因，Z世代用户认为，评级较低的应用程序将自动从应用程序商店中删除。这是不正确的。）当时的努力使该应用程序的整体星级降低到仅1.2星，最终迫使特朗普2020年竞选活动重置该应用的评级。
 尽管选举已经结束，但用户仍然在应用程序上留下差评以及他们的1星级评分。有时，巨魔甚至会在过程中尝试一些幽默。
 一位Play商店评论员说：“ App试图发动政变，以推翻我手机的操作系统。” “自2016年以来，我受够了痛苦，”另一位在iOS上表示。
note_en:
The Play Store version hadn’t been updated since October. 30, 2020, for example, according to data from Sensor Tower.
 According to Android Police’s report, the app was hanging and couldn’t load content, and it reported connectivity issues. We understand the issue was as they described — when users downloaded the app, it would either hang on the initial loading screen with a spinning “T” logo or it would immediately report a server error at startup. In either case, it would never load the app experience at all.
  Recent user reviews on the Play Store noted these issues, saying things like “ will not open,” “the app doesn’t even work,” “absolutely terrible doesn’t even work,” “wouldn’t open keep saying check connections,” and more. One user even asked the developer to respond to the numerous complaints, saying  “please reply to people commenting. It’s not loading.” Another implied the issues were Google’s fault, noting  “worked great, until Google canceled it.”
 Google, though, did not cancel it. The Trump 2020 Android app has actually been experiencing problems for some time before Google took this action.
 For example, a tweet from around a month ago described a similar set of issues:
  Google told with TechCrunch the app has not been banned from the Play Store, only suspended for its non-functionality. It can be reinstated if the problems are addressed. The company also said it attempted to reach out to the app’s developer before taking the app down, but never received a response.
 “The Trump 2020 campaign app recently stopped working and we reached out to the developer multiple times in an attempt to get them to address the issue,” a Google spokesperson said. “People expect that apps downloaded from Google Play provide a minimum level of functionality and our policy is to remove non-working apps from the store if they are not fixed.”
 Despite the issues on Android, we found the iOS version was still able to load upon first launch, and could send confirmation codes to a phone number at sign-up. But when you visited the app’s main screens, it also now presents an error message. This error doesn’t affect your ability to browse through the past content on iOS, however.
  According to date from Sensor Tower, the Android version hadn’t seen any new installs since Feb. 7, 2021. The firm also noted the Trump 2020 Android app had around 840,000 installs compared with close to 1.5 million on iOS.
 This is not the first time the Trump 2020 app’s issues have made headlines.
 In the months leading up to last year’s presidential election in the U.S., a number of TikTok users  decided to troll the app in its user reviews. (For some reason, Gen Z users believe a lowly-rated app will be automatically removed from the app stores. That’s not true.) Their efforts at the time were able to bring the app’s overall star rating down to just 1.2 stars, and eventually forced the Trump 2020 campaign  to reset the app’s ratings.
 Though the election is long over, users have still been leaving bad reviews on the app along with their 1-star ratings. Sometimes, the trolls even attempt a bit of humor in the process.
 “App attempted a coup to overthrow my phone’s operating system,” said one Play Store reviewer. “I’ve suffered enough since 2016,” said another on iOS.
title: 谷歌因无法正常运行而从Play商店暂停Trump 2020应用
title_en: Google suspends Trump 2020 app from Play Store for non-functionality

note:
作为音频设备（主要是效果踏板，旧式磁带机和我修改过的1980年代键盘）的收集者，我希望我可以说我的播客设置中包含非常昂贵且难以获得的设备。我想炫耀自己使用手动接线的精品前置放大器和我在哈萨克斯坦的一次军事拍卖中购买的罕见的苏联电容麦克风。共享一台大型卷对卷磁带机的照片，然后在笔记本电脑上混合使用之前，我记录了我的广告读物（“保暖”），这对我来说无非是一件好事。
 las，我的播客设置非常正常。我在一家连锁商店购买了Scarlett两通道界面。我有一个Rode麦克风，因为我买不起Shure SM7B。我记录到GarageBand，这是音频接口的螺旋式笔记本。关于播客设置，唯一有点不寻常的事情是，在极少数情况下，我在Ableton Live中编辑情节时，这是我几年前最初痴迷于制作mashup时最初购买的。
  我只能说是一种可耻的模仿：我的笔记本电脑太旧了，USB端口似乎变得松弛了-令我惊讶的是，它们没有像老人的耳朵那样长出头发-所以我必须修复用电工胶带将我的斯嘉丽的电线固定到位。
 Election Profit Makers是有关使用PredictIt.org网站投注政治事件的播客。 （我的共同主持人乔恩·金博尔（Jon Kimball）在2020年选举中赚了足够的钱来购买新车；我赚了足够的钱来购买新的颤音踏板。）我们做现场录音的唯一时间是去年春天，那时乔恩和我继续COVID袭击当周在加勒比海的书呆子/喜剧巡游。我们使用Zoom H4N记录了海上的日常派遣，然后在圣多明各徘徊，直到找到一个大学图书馆，该图书馆的Wi-Fi可用于将文件上传到我们的共同主持人和编辑Starlee。我的电话告诉我那天我走了24,000步。
  因为我的播客设置太无聊了，所以我在镜头中加入了其他音频设备，为照片增添了趣味！当世界为模拟延迟饱和的盒式播客做好准备时，我将非常乐意承担！
note_en:
As a collector of audio gear (mostly effects pedals, old rim-drive tape machines and 1980s keyboards I’ve modified), I wish I could say my podcasting setup featured equipment that is extremely expensive and hard to come by. I would love to brag about using, say, hand-wired boutique preamps and a rare Soviet condenser microphone I bought at a military auction in Kazakhstan. Nothing would please me more than to share photographs of a massive reel-to-reel tape machine on which I record my ad reads (for “warmth”) before mixing them down on my laptop.
 Alas, my podcasting setup is extremely normal. I have a Scarlett two-channel interface I bought at a chain store. I have a Rode microphone because I couldn’t afford a Shure SM7B. I record into GarageBand, which is the spiral-bound notebook of audio interfaces. The only slightly unusual thing about my podcasting setup is that on the rare occasion when I edit an episode I do so in Ableton Live, which I originally bought years ago when I was obsessed with making mashups.
  The only analog affectation I can claim is a shameful one: My laptop is so old the USB ports seem to be going slack — I’m surprised they don’t have hair growing out of them like old men’s ears — so I have to fix the line from my Scarlett into place using electrical tape.
 Election Profit Makers is a podcast about betting on political events using the web site  PredictIt.org. (My co-host Jon Kimball made enough money on the 2020 election to buy a new car; I made enough to buy a new tremolo pedal.) The only time we’ve done field recordings was last spring, when Jon and I went on a nerd / comedy cruise in the Caribbean the week COVID hit. We recorded daily dispatches at sea using a Zoom H4N, then wandered around Santo Domingo until we found a university library whose Wi-Fi we could use to upload the files to our co-host and editor Starlee. My phone tells me I walked 24,000 steps that day.
  Because my podcasting setup is so boring, I have spiced up the photos by including some of my other audio gear in the shots! When the world is ready for cassette-based podcasts saturated in analog delay, I will be more than happy to oblige!
title: 我的播客方式：选举获利者的David Rees
title_en: How I Podcast: Election Profit Makers’ David Rees

note:
自9月以来，来自保守媒体人物，新闻媒体和支持Donald Trump的页面的帖子占Facebook上表现最好的链接的68％。
  丹·邦吉诺（Dan Bongino）在许多方面都是完美的保守强人。肌肉发达，气势磅his，他只是在右翼得分文化战争中的存在，而与左翼大豆男生的意识形态形成鲜明对比。他的个人资料吹嘘他是《纽约时报》的畅销书作家和前特勤局特工，以前与总统职位很近，这使他在谈论政治时具有公信力。
 他还是错误信息的泛滥者。他最畅销的书《间谍门：唐纳德·特朗普的企图破坏》激增了被驳斥的阴谋论，即巴拉克·奥巴马和希拉里·克林顿使用间谍试图破坏特朗普2016年的竞选活动。他对COVID-19大流行开始时口罩的有效性表示怀疑。他的专页是2020年大选阴谋论的中心。早在2月10日，邦吉诺（Bongino）便在他的Facebook页面上发布文章，声称民主党人在特朗普的弹each审判中使用篡改的镜头，这一说法很快被驳斥。
 尽管有这些事情，或更可能是因为这些事情，但Bongino还是Facebook上最受欢迎的人物之一。他的页面有超过200万个赞和340万个关注者，其帖子链接到他的现场表演和网站，始终是该网站上效果最佳的链接。但是Bongino并不是唯一一个在Facebook上发布错误信息并蓬勃发展的保守人物。
 自2020年7月22日以来，《纽约时报》专栏作家凯文·罗斯（Kevin Roose）一直在使用Facebook拥有的分析服务Crowdtangle在Facebook上分享性能最高的前10个链接，该报道由Facebook拥有。利用Roose的数据，商务部追踪了自9月28日以来表现最好的链接，那是在唐纳德·特朗普和乔·拜登之间进行首次电视辩论的前一天。数据显示，在整个选举季节以及最近的上周，保守派媒体人物发布了散布错误信息的历史信息，一直是该网站上效果最好的链接。
 实际上，保守派媒体页面上的帖子（包括Bongino和Ben Shapiro这样的人物，福克斯新闻和Newsmax这样的新闻媒体以及唐纳德·特朗普等支持唐纳德·特朗普的页面，例如唐纳德·特朗普的美国爱国者）在880名榜首在记录的时间内在Facebook上执行链接。
 自9月28日以来，并非每天都有跟踪记录。自我们开始计数以来的最近137天中，仅提供了88个性能最高的链接列表。下面的竞赛图显示了在记录的每一天哪些页面具有性能最高的链接，以及该页面上有多少帖子在前10名中 
在整个记录的时期内，保守派页面表现良好，尤其是在发生重大政治事件的当天或之后。
 9月29日，即第一次总统辩论的那天，前10名的最佳表现链接中有9条来自保守派网页，包括福克斯新闻，丹·邦吉诺，本·夏皮罗，唐纳德·特朗普和唐纳德·特朗普的美国爱国者-此趋势在接下来的两个月中继续辩论。在副总统辩论之日，来自Bongino的帖子构成了Facebook上表现最佳的链接的一半。在第二次总统辩论中，邦吉诺（Bongino）与福克斯新闻（Fox News）和美国有线电视新闻网（CNN）的另外两个表现最佳。
 在选举日，Bongino，Trump，Ben＆amp; Dr.糖果卡森（Candy Carson）和福克斯新闻（Fox News）拥有7个表现最佳的链接，其他3个分别属于CNN和亚历山大·奥卡西奥·科尔特斯（Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez）。在11月7日-美联社和其他多家媒体开始选举乔·拜登的那天-唐纳德·特朗普是唯一一位保守派人物，与前十名有联系，其他人都属于CNN，NPR，纽约时报和自由派专家瑞秋·马多（Rachel Maddow）。
 但是，保守派媒体只花了一天的时间就恢复了其在大多数表现最好的职位中的位置。到11月9日，唐纳德·特朗普（Donald Trump）占据了前十名的五个职位，其他人则属于丹·邦吉诺（Dan Bongino），宗教保守派和特朗普的支持者富兰克林·格雷厄姆（Franklin Graham）和Newsmax。 Twitter和Facebook在1月8日暂停了唐纳德·特朗普的账户和其他保守账户，这只会助长火势-仅在一天后的1月9日，邦吉诺，夏皮罗，福克斯新闻，布里特巴特和布里吉特·加布里埃尔排名前10位的链接中的七个。
   尽管在记录的时间段内，有103页的链接在前10名中排名最高，但在Facebook上具有最高性能链接的20页中，有最多的表格显示了保守媒体在平台上的支配地位。 Bongino不仅在保守派人物中而且在整个Facebook上都占据了榜首。在记录的88天中，有84天出现了来自Bongino页面的链接，总共有197个链接。链接量排名前10位的页面是福克斯新闻（117），其次是本·夏皮罗（Ben Shapiro），排名70.在链接表现最好的103页中，有67个页面每天仅出现一个或两个链接前10名。
 表现最好的保守派媒体联系主要来自像Bongino和Shapiro这样的专家以及像Ted Cruz这样的政客，而来自保守派媒体和专页对唐纳德·特朗普的明确支持的则略少。 CNN，NPR和《纽约时报》是在记录的时间段内表现最出色的前十个页面中仅有的非保守媒体，分别拥有67个，33个和20个表现最佳的链接。罗伯特·赖希（Robert Reich）是表现最好的民主党专家，在记录的88天内只有10个表现最好的职位。
 本·沙皮罗（Ben Shapiro）是Brietbart的前总编辑，也是保守媒体网站The Daily Wire的创建者，以其有争议的信念，书籍和节目《本·夏皮罗表演》而在整个社交媒体上广为人知。过去，夏皮罗（Shapiro）提出了几项可疑的说法，例如他错误地宣称世界上一半以上的穆斯林人口已经激进化。夏皮罗（Shapiro）是许多创作者之一，包括史蒂文·克劳德（Steven Crowder），戴夫·鲁宾（Dave Rubin）以及其他人，这些人组成了曾经被称为＆＃34;知识暗网的＆＃34;一群挑战自由主义和民主信仰的右翼专家。这些创建者（包括夏皮罗本人）中的许多人都拒绝了极右运动和白人至上主义运动，但研究表明，他们的内容通常是通向在线极右社区的直接渠道。保守派媒体Newsmax最近也收到警告，称它可能会面临Dominion Voting Systems的诽谤诉讼，理由是该公司助长了针对唐纳德·特朗普的选举。 
从实现机器学习以识别虚假故事到调查病毒性故事的准确性（例如Hunter Biden笔记本电脑丑闻本身），Facebook一直在努力遏制该平台上的错误信息，但收效甚微。错误信息和极端内容在新的拜登政府领导下的平台上与去年大选期间一样普遍。Facebook才刚刚开始限制用户中政治内容的出现。整体新闻源。
 周三，Facebook宣布将根据一项拟议的法律从澳大利亚平台上删除所有新闻内容，这将要求该公司为发布在Facebook上的每篇文章向出版商付款。 Facbeook在报告中指出，该公司的业务收益微乎其微，新闻所占的比例不到用户所看到的4％。新闻提要。但是，Facebook并未提供对它认为是＆＃34; news＆＃34;的定义。内容。来自Bongino，Shapiro和其他人的帖子可能不会被严格视为＆＃34; news＆＃34;通过Facebook，但他们经常这样打扮自己，从视觉上看与普通新闻几乎没有区别。对他们的追随者而言，Bongino和Shapiro的帖子被认为比来自主流媒体的可信度更高–皮尤研究中心（Pew Research Center）2020年的一项调查发现，五分之一的美国成年人主要通过新闻获取新闻社交媒体，这些成年人通常较少了解信息或更容易受到错误信息的影响。不管4％的数字是否正确，在记录的期间内，张贴政治或新闻内容的Facebook页面均占前20个最佳页面中的17个。在链接性能方面，按大多数标准可以被视为新闻源的Fox News是Facebook上第二受欢迎的页面。不论Facebook是否从新闻内容中获得重大业务收益，其在平台上的盛行和成功都足以支持整个业务和生态系统，例如Bongino和其他智能暗网。 ;数字。
 Bongino本人不确定他为什么在Facebook上如此受欢迎。 “我们不使用机器人。我们甚至没有在Facebook上打出很多广告，” Bongino在十月份的一次采访中对罗斯说。 “如果我告诉你在过去的一年中我花了10分钟进行分析，那我会撒谎。”
 Bongino的许多内容都在叙述中，保守派正被政客和科技平台压制。当Twitter在1月8日禁止Donald Trump的帐户时，Bongino陷入了交火。他的帐户被暂停了12个小时，这促使他完全出于抗议行动而离开了平台。 Bongino告诉Roose，他也正出于意识形态原因也准备从Facebook撤职，Facebook最近决定限制新闻源中的政治内容数量可能最终削弱Bongino的帝国。但是与此同时，他和他的专家们正在蓬勃发展，并且几乎可以完全控制哪些链接在世界上最大的社交媒体网络上获得最多的氧气。
    Thinknum会使用在线发布的信息，工作，社交和网络流量，产品销售以及应用评级来跟踪公司，并创建可衡量诸如雇用，收入和人流量等因素的数据集。数据集可能并不完全全面（它们仅考虑了网络上可用的内容），但是它们可用于评估人员和销售等绩效因素。
note_en:
Since September, posts from conservative media figures, news outlets and pages in support of Donald Trump made up for 68% of the top performing links on Facebook.
  Dan Bongino is, in many ways, the perfect conservative strongman. Muscular and imposing, his mere existence as a voice on the right scores culture war points against the perceived droves of left-wing soyboys. His profiles boast that he is a New York Times bestselling author and former Secret Service agent, a former proximity to the presidency which affords him an air of credibility when talking politics.
 He is also a rampant purveyor of misinformation. His bestselling book,   Spygate: The Attempted Sabotage of Donald Trump  proliferates the disproven conspiracy theory that Barack Obama and Hillary Clinton used spies to attempt to sabotage Trump’s 2016 campaign. He cast doubt over the effectiveness of masks at the start of the COVID-19 pandemic. His page was a hub of 2020 election conspiracy theories. As recently as February 10, Bongino posted on his Facebook page claiming that Democrats used doctored footage in Trump’s impeachment trial, a claim which was   quickly disproved .
 Despite, or more likely because of all these things, Bongino is one of the most popular figures on Facebook. His page has over 2 million likes and over 3.4 million followers, and its posts, which link to his live show and website, consistently rank among the top-performing links on the site. But Bongino is not the only conservative figure posting misinformation and thriving on Facebook.
 Kevin Roose, a columnist at the New York Times   who has covered Bongino , has been using the Facebook-owned analytics service Crowdtangle to share the top 10 highest-performing links on Facebook since July 22, 2020   on Twitter . Using Roose’s data, the Business of Business tracked the top-performing link since September 28 — the day before the first televised debate between Donald Trump and Joe Biden took place. The data revealed that throughout the election season and as recently as last week, posts from conservative media figures with histories of spreading misinformation have consistently ranked among the best performing links on the site.
 In fact, posts from conservative media pages — including figures like Bongino and Ben Shapiro, news outlets such as Fox News and Newsmax, and pages in support of Donald Trump such as USA Patriots for Donald Trump — made up for 68% of the 880 top performing links on Facebook during the recorded period.
 Not every single day since September 28 has been tracked; of the last 137 days since our count began, the list of top-performing links was only provided for 88. The race chart below shows which pages had top-performing links on every recorded day, as well as how many posts from that page were in the top 10.
   Conservative pages performed well throughout the entire recorded period — most notably on the days of or after major political events took place.
 On September 29, the day of the first Presidential debate, nine of the top 10 performing links came from conservative pages including Fox News, Dan Bongino, Ben Shapiro, Donald Trump and USA Patriots for Donald Trump — a trend which continued for the following two debates. On the day of the Vice Presidential Debate, posts from Bongino made up half of the top-performing links on Facebook. By the second Presidential debate, Bongino had eight of the top performing links with the other two belonging to Fox News and CNN.
 On election day, posts from Bongino, Trump, Dr. Ben & Candy Carson, and Fox News had seven of the top performing links, with the other three belonging to CNN and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez. On November 7 — the day the Associated Press and several other media outlets began calling the election for Joe Biden — Donald Trump was the lone conservative figure with a link in the top 10, with the others belonging to news outlets like CNN, NPR, The New York Times and liberal pundit Rachel Maddow.
 However, it only took one day for conservative media to reclaim its spot with the majority of the top-performing posts. By November 9, Donald Trump dominated the top 10 with five posts, with others belonging to Dan Bongino, religious conservative and Trump supporter Franklin Graham, and Newsmax — the only odd one out was a single post from then Vice President-Elect Kamala Harris. Twitter and Facebook's suspensions of Donald Trump's accounts and other conservative accounts on January 8 only added fuel to the fire — just one day later on January 9, Bongino, Shapiro, Fox News, Brietbart and Brigitte Gabriel had seven of the top 10 performing links.
   Though 103 pages had links in the top 10 during the recorded period, a table of the 20 pages with the highest volume of top-performing links on Facebook shows the chokehold conservative media has on the platform. Bongino dominates the list not just among conservative figures but across all of Facebook. Links from Bongino’s page appeared in 84 of the 88 recorded days, with a total count of 197 links. The page with the next-highest volume of links in the top 10 was Fox News with 117, followed by Ben Shapiro with 70. Of the 103 pages with top-performing links, 67 of them only had one or two links appear in a daily top 10.
 The majority of the top-performing conservative media links came from pundits like Bongino and Shapiro as well as from politicians like Ted Cruz, with slightly less  coming from conservative media outlets and pages in explicit support of Donald Trump. CNN, NPR and The New York Times are the only non-conservative media outlets to be among the top 10 performing pages during the recorded period with 67, 33 and 20  of the top-performing links respectively.  Robert Reich was the top-performing Democratic pundit with only 10  top-performing posts during the 88 recorded days.
 Ben Shapiro, former editor-at-large of Brietbart and creator of conservative media site The Daily Wire, is well-known across social media for his controversial beliefs, books and show,   The Ben Shapiro Show . In the past, Shapiro has made several dubious claims, such as when he   falsely argued  that over half of the world’s Muslim population was radicalized. Shapiro is one of many creators including Steven Crowder, Dave Rubin and others who make up what was once referred to as the "intellectual dark web," a group of right-wing pundits who challenged liberal and democratic beliefs. Many of these creators including Shapiro himself have disavowed the alt-right movement and white supremacist movements, yet  studies show their content is often a direct pipeline to online alt-right communities. Conservative media outlet Newsmax also recently received warning that it could face a defamation lawsuit from Dominion Voting Systems for false claims that the company helped rig the election against Donald Trump.
 Facebook has made repeated attempts to curb misinformation on the platform, from implementing machine learning to identify false stories to investigating the accuracy of viral stories like the Hunter Biden laptop scandal itself, but to little avail. Misinformation and extreme content is as prevalent on the platform under the new Biden administration as it was during election season last year, and Facebook is  only now beginning to restrict the presence of political content in users' newsfeeds overall.
 On Wednesday, Facebook announced that it would  remove all news content from the platform in Australia in response to a proposed law which would require the company to pay publishers for every article posted on Facebook. In it, Facbeook stated that the company's business gain is minimal, and that news makes up less than 4% of what appears in users' news feeds. However, Facebook did not provide a definition of what it considers to be "news" content. Posts from Bongino, Shapiro and others may not be considered strictly "news" by Facebook, but they often dress themselves up as such, looking nearly indistinguishable visually from average news articles. To their followers, Bongino's and Shapiro's posts are viewed as more credible than if they came from a mainstream media outlet — A 2020 survey from Pew Research Center found that one in five U.S. adults get their news primarily through social media, and that these adults are typically less informed or more susceptible to misinformation. Regardless of whether the 4% figure is correct, Facebook pages that post political or news content made up for 17 of the top 20 performing pages during the recorded period. Fox News, which can be considered a news source by most standards, is the second-most popular page on Facebook in terms of link performance. Regardless of whether Facebook has a significant business benefit from news content or not, the prevalence and success of it on the platform is enough to support entire businesses and ecosystems, like Bongino's and other "intellectual dark web" figures.
 Bongino himself isn’t sure why he’s so popular on Facebook. “We don’t use bots. We don’t even advertise much on Facebook,” Bongino told Roose in an October interview. “If I told you I spent 10 minutes on analytics over the past year, I’d be lying.”
 Much of Bongino’s content plays into the narrative that conservatives are being silenced by politicians and tech platforms. When Twitter banned Donald Trump’s account on January 8, Bongino was wrapped up in the crossfire. His account was suspended for 12 hours, which prompted him to leave the platform entirely in an act of protest. Bongino told Roose that he is preparing to be removed from Facebook for ideological reasons as well, and Facebook's recent decision to curb the amount of political content in newsfeeds may end up denting Bongino's empire. But in the meantime, he and his fellow pundits are thriving and have a near-complete control over which links get the most oxygen on the world’s largest social media network.
    Thinknum tracks companies using the information they post online, jobs, social and web traffic, product sales, and app ratings, and creates data sets that measure factors like hiring, revenue, and foot traffic. Data sets may not be fully comprehensive (they only account for what is available on the web), but they can be used to gauge performance factors like staffing and sales.
title: 自9月以来，Facebook上68％的顶级链接都支持唐纳德·特朗普（Donald Trump）
title_en: 68% of the top links on Facebook since September are in support of Donald Trump

Waymo的故事始于旧金山湾区。自2009年初以来，我们在加利福尼亚州完成了最初的1,000英里自动驾驶里程后，便开始了该地区的延伸和广度，对三藩市及周边地区的诸多独特挑战已非常熟悉。我们还与社区的各个成员紧密合作，从地方当局和社区团体到道路使用者，我们加深了对城市居民需求的了解。例如，在2020年秋天，我们对近1,000名旧金山居民进行了调查，以了解他们的交通需求。当被问及导致绕城难行的因素时，有63％的受访者指出危险的驾驶员，有74％的人指出停车，有57％的人指出有压力的通勤。令人担忧的是，将近四分之一的人根本不在旧金山的道路上感到安全。自动驾驶汽车有望改变这一现状，通过使交通更安全，更便宜，更便捷地进入城市来彻底改变我们的城市。但是，如果不深入了解自动驾驶技术必须应对的挑战的性质，就不可能部署自动驾驶技术（可能是我们这个时代最大的工程问题之一）。构建用于城市驾驶的最先进的技术栈在旧金山，这意味着应对该市标志性的地形变化–从起伏的丘陵到沙质的海洋高速公路，狭窄的小路到巨大的高速公路，自行车道到电车道，以及介于两者之间的所有事物。这也意味着学习如何安全处理城市的其他道路使用者。旧金山的街道上到处都是交通，行人，骑自行车的人，踏板车和紧急车辆-更不用说定义城市景观的缆车，手推车，有轨电车和轻轨车辆了。建立能够安全有效地解决这一复杂问题的自动驾驶服务是一项巨大的工程挑战。在过去的十年中，我们已经在数十个城市对我们的技术进行了测试，以建立业内最成熟的技术体系-一种足以支持美国亚利桑那州凤凰城唯一的公共商业乘车服务的技术，而无需人工控制车内或远程车辆。在这段时间里，我们一直在调整技术，以在旧金山等人口稠密的城市中行驶。仅举几个例子：我们优化了Waymo Driver的360°视觉系统和激光雷达，以应对城市驾驶的复杂性。我们的高灵敏度摄像头可以发现长距离变化的交通信号灯-甚至在24街的papel picado中-都能确保平稳行驶。而且我们的相机和激光雷达可以立即发现行人专用道上的冲刺，并采取适当的行动-即使它们突然从对面车道的车辆后面出现。
 我们还设计了软件来推理环境，这对于在繁忙的城市中安全行驶至关重要。我们的感知系统使驾驶员知道如何处理行人，树木和行人携带圣诞树。如果我们在渔人码头的海滩街上的人行横道旁乘公共汽车出站，我们的驾驶员会推断出隐藏的乘客可能正在下车，并且他们可能很快就会过马路。
 我们还在驾驶软件中增加了灵活性，以应对道路意外变化。如果我们在道路工作期间在第19大街上行驶并且我们的传感器发现了交通锥和道路工作标志，则我们的感知系统会了解到它们正在引导我们驶离通常的车道，并且我们的规划和路线选择系统可以自动将车辆的路线更新为浏览新布局。
 不断改进我们的技术我们现在正在加快城市中技术的开发和测试，因此我们有一天可以将自动驾驶的好处带给更多的人。在我们部署商业服务之前，我们的团队会提供有关产品体验的反馈，从而使我们能够验证正在取得的进步并继续改善骑手的体验。现在，我们已经在Waymo的员工志愿者中开始了在旧金山的限量骑士测试，以收集反馈并继续改进我们的技术。我们正在使用增强型COVID-19协议进行这项测试，以确保每个参与者的安全。在接下来的几个月中，我们将分享有关如何优化技术以解决旧金山驾驶问题的更多详细信息。
note_en:
Waymo’s story starts in the San Francisco Bay Area. Since early 2009, when we completed our first 1,000 autonomous miles across California, we’ve driven the length and breadth of the region, becoming intimately familiar with the many unique challenges of driving in San Francisco and the surrounding area.     We’ve also worked closely with various members of the community, from local authorities and community groups to our fellow road-users, to deepen our understanding of the needs of the city’s residents. For example, in fall 2020, we surveyed nearly 1,000 San Franciscans to understand their transportation needs. When asked to name factors making it hard to get around the city, 63 percent of respondents pointed to dangerous drivers, 74 percent to parking and 57 percent to stressful commutes. Worryingly, nearly a quarter didn't feel safe on San Francisco’s roads at all. Autonomous vehicles promise to change this, revolutionizing our cities by making transport safer, cheaper, and easier to access. But deploying autonomous driving technology to get there – perhaps one of the greatest engineering problems of our time – isn’t possible without developing a deep understanding of the nature of the challenges it must master.   Building the most advanced technology stack for urban driving  In San Francisco, that means tackling the city’s iconic topographical variety – from rolling hills to sandy ocean highways, tiny side streets to huge freeways, bike lanes to tram tracks, and everything in between.  It also means learning how to handle the city’s other road users safely. San Francisco’s streets are busy with traffic, pedestrians, cyclists, scooters, and emergency vehicles – and that’s not to mention the cable cars, trolleys, streetcars and light rail vehicles that define the cityscape.  Building an autonomous driving service that can tackle this complexity safely and effectively is an enormous engineering challenge. Over the past decade, we’ve tested our technology in dozens of cities to build up the most mature technology stack in the industry – one advanced enough to power  the US’s only public commercial ride-hailing service in Phoenix, Arizona, with no human controlling the vehicles either in-car or remotely.  Throughout that time, we’ve also been gearing our technology to drive in dense cities such as San Francisco. To give just a few examples:  We’ve optimized the Waymo Driver’s 360° vision system and lidar to navigate the complexities of urban driving. Our highly sensitive cameras can spot traffic lights changing at a long distance – even among the papel picado on 24th Street – to enable smooth driving. And our cameras and lidar can instantly spot a jaywalker sprinting across our path and act appropriately – even when they emerge suddenly from behind a vehicle in the oncoming lane.
 We’ve also designed our software to reason about the context, which is essential for driving safely in busy cities. Our perception system lets our Driver know how to handle a pedestrian, a tree – and a pedestrian carrying a Christmas tree. If we pull up next to a bus by a crosswalk on Beach Street in Fisherman’s Wharf, our Driver can reason that hidden passengers may be getting off, and that they may soon cross the street.
 We’re also building greater flexibility into our driving software to handle unexpected changes to the road. If we’re driving on 19th Avenue during road work and our sensors spot traffic cones and road work signs, our perception system understands that they are guiding us out of the usual lane, and our planning and routing systems can automatically update the vehicle’s route to navigate the new layout.
 Making continual improvements to our technology We’re now accelerating the development and testing of our technology in cities, so we can one day bring the benefits of fully autonomous driving to more people.  Well before we deploy a commercial service, our team provides feedback on the product experience, allowing us to validate the progress we’re making and continue improving the rider experience. We’ve now started limited rider testing in San Francisco with Waymo employee volunteers to gather feedback and continue to improve our technology. We’re conducting this testing with enhanced COVID-19 protocols to ensure the safety of everyone involved.  Over the next few months, we’ll share further details about how we’re optimizing our technologies to tackle driving in San Francisco.
title: Waymo：扩展我们在旧金山的测试
title_en: Waymo: Expanding our testing in San Francisco

为了响应莫里森政府拟议的媒体议价法，Facebook做出了惊人的举动，禁止澳大利亚的出版商和用户共享或查看其平台上的任何新闻报道。
 该议事规则已于周三晚上在众议院通过，将要求社交媒体公司向媒体机构支付使用其内容的费用。预计该法案最早将在下周通过，并成为法律。
  Facebook在周四（AEDT）的博客文章中表示，在与澳大利亚政府的讨论中找不到解决方案之后，它继续进行了9月份的威胁。
 从星期四初起，用户就无法使用《悉尼先驱晨报》和《时代》等新闻网站的Facebook页面上的内容，以及《澳大利亚人》和《先驱太阳报》等新闻集团的报纸，以及所有ABC内容。在线青年出版物Junkee Media和讽刺新闻网站The Betoota Advocate和The Chaser所发表的文章也被删除。由新闻媒体经营的一些Facebook团体上没有文章。
  这项禁令似乎还击垮了政府机构的Facebook页面，其中包括气象局，SA Health，ACT Health和昆士兰健康局。悉尼几个地方卫生区的页面也被擦掉了，墨尔本的皇家儿童医院也被擦掉了。
  Facebook澳大利亚和新西兰董事总经理威廉·伊斯顿说：“拟议的法律从根本上误解了我们的平台与使用该平台共享新闻内容的发行商之间的关系。”
 “这使我们面临一个严峻的选择：试图遵守无视这种关系现实的法律，或者停止在我们的澳大利亚服务中允许新闻内容。怀着沉重的心情，我们选择后者。 
“不幸的是，这意味着澳大利亚的人们和新闻组织现在被限制发布新闻链接以及在Facebook上共享或查看澳大利亚和国际新闻内容。”
  通讯部长保罗·弗莱彻（Paul Fletcher）在回应周四上午的Facebook公告时表示，政府不会拒绝对该法规进行立法。
 “我们将继续遵循的道路。总理，司库和我对此一直持一致态度，”弗莱彻先生告诉2GB电台。
 他说，Facebook阻止权威新闻来源的决定将使该公司进一步遭受错误信息和未经验证内容的传播。
 “在澳大利亚拥有多元化且资源丰富的新闻媒体部门非常重要，这是我们民主制度的重要组成部分。现在，这对于硅谷的一家公司而言似乎并不重要，但对澳大利亚政府和澳大利亚人民来说却非常重要。”弗莱彻先生说。
 “如果您正在寻找可靠的新闻，他们实际上是对澳大利亚人说的，Facebook不是寻找它的地方。”
  工党在周三投票支持众议院的这一法案，周四表示，政府未能提供“可行的法案”。 
通讯发言人米歇尔·罗兰德（Michelle Rowland）说：“这不是本届政府制定的可行代码。”
 “本届政府有责任解释它的去向。现实是，过去两周来他们一直在谈论世界上最大的游戏。”
 该禁令似乎已经对至少一些用户生效，这些用户现在无法在其Facebook页面上发布澳大利亚新闻文章的链接，也无法查看主要媒体公司过去发布的文章。伊斯顿的博客说，不仅澳大利亚的媒体在分享Facebook页面上的内容受到限制，而且澳大利亚用户也无法查看或转发来自《华尔街日报》和《纽约时报》等报纸的国际文章。
  发言人说：“这一举动再次证明了他们的垄断地位和不合理的行为。” “但是今天的声明并不意味着Facebook不必遵守联邦政府的提议法规。价值已经转移，Facebook多年来从我们的内容中受益。”
 “我们应该能够访问他们的垄断平台，并因此有权通过我们的内容获利。”
 美国广播公司（ABC）董事总经理大卫·安德森（David Anderson）说，这家国家广播公司受到了影响，正在与Facebook讨论这一变化。
 财政部长乔什·弗莱登伯格（Josh Frydenberg）与该公司全球首席执行官马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）进行了多次讨论之后，Facebook做出了这一决定。直到周日，谈判仍在进行，而弗莱登贝格先生此前曾称其为“建设性的”。 
1月底，弗莱登贝格在ABC的Insiders计划中说：“马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）并没有说服我退缩，如果那是您的要求，”
 但是，熟悉政府与Facebook讨论的业内消息人士称，弗莱登贝格没有得到有关Facebook应对其今天威胁的计划的任何有意义的通知。
 弗赖登贝格先生今天早上在推特上说，他刚刚与扎克伯格谈过话。弗莱登伯格写道：“我们同意继续进行对话，以寻求前进的道路。”
  当前与Facebook有关系并在其feed中发布文章的新闻媒体在周四清晨被告知其内容将受到影响。
  《悉尼先驱晨报》和《时代报》看到，Facebook澳大利亚和新西兰新闻合作伙伴负责人安德鲁·亨特（Andrew Hunter）发出的一封信说，该技术平台“感到失望”，因为它必须坚决应对威胁，并且会吸引新闻。网点进一步讨论未来几天的变化。
  Facebook正在与该刊头的所有者Nine Entertainment Co和新闻集团澳大利亚等新闻媒体就过去两周内对其内容的付款进行谈判。
 但是，由于几个关键点，这些谈判在过去五天陷入了停顿，包括Facebook合同中的明文规定，如果有新法律颁布，这将使它炸毁任何交易。造成这种情况的主要原因是，不遵守该守则可能会使Facebook处以高达当地收入10％的罚款。 
伊斯顿表示，政府拟议中的立法“旨在对Facebook没收或索取的内容进行处罚”。
  他说：“这项立法为政府决定谁签署这些新闻内容协议树立了先例，最终决定了已经从免费服务中获得价值的一方获得了多少报酬。”
 “作为我们计划投资新的许可新闻计划和经验的计划的一部分，我们现在将把投资重点放在其他国家。”
 Facebook的立场代表了与搜索巨头谷歌不同的做法，谷歌也曾威胁要退出澳大利亚。此后，Google已与澳大利亚主要发行商达成数百万美元的交易，以使用其内容。
 谷歌已同意向拥有《悉尼先驱晨报》和《时代》的Nine Entertainment公司支付每年超过3000万美元的现金，以用于其新闻内容的使用。该公司还与Seven West Media达成了类似的交易，并在一夜之间宣布了与新闻集团的全球交易。
  Zoe Samios是《悉尼先驱晨报》和《时代》的媒体和电信记者。
 丽莎·维森汀（Lisa Visentin）是《悉尼先驱晨报》和《时代》杂志的联邦政治记者，内容涵盖教育和传播。
note_en:
Facebook has made a stunning move to ban publishers and users in Australia from sharing or viewing any news articles on its platform in response to the Morrison government’s proposed media bargaining laws.
 The bargaining code,  which passed through the House of Representatives on Wednesday night, will require social media companies to pay media outlets for using their content. The bill is expected to pass the Senate and become law as early as next week.
  In  a blog post on Thursday (AEDT) Facebook said that it had carried through on the  threat made in September after being unable to find a solution in discussions with the Australian government.
 Content on Facebook pages from news websites such as  The Sydney Morning Herald and  The Age, News Corp newspapers such as  The Australian and  The Herald Sun and all ABC content were made unavailable to users from early Thursday. Articles published by online youth publication Junkee Media and satirical news websites  The Betoota Advocate and  The Chaser were also removed. Some Facebook groups run by news outlets have no articles on them.
  The ban also appears to have struck down the Facebook pages of government agencies, including the Bureau of Meteorology, SA Health, ACT Health and Queensland Health. The pages of several Sydney local health districts have also been wiped, as has the Royal Children’s Hospital in Melbourne.
  “The proposed law fundamentally misunderstands the relationship between our platform and publishers who use it to share news content,” William Easton, Facebook’s Australia and New Zealand managing director, said.
 “It has left us facing a stark choice: attempt to comply with a law that ignores the realities of this relationship, or stop allowing news content on our services in Australia. With a heavy heart, we are choosing the latter.
 “Unfortunately, this means people and news organisations in Australia are now restricted from posting news links and sharing or viewing Australian and international news content on Facebook.”
  Responding to Facebook’s announcement on Thursday morning, Communications Minister Paul Fletcher said the government would not back down from legislating the code.
 “We’ll be maintaining the path that we’ve been following. The Prime Minister, the Treasurer and I have been very consistent on that,” Mr Fletcher told 2GB radio.
 He said Facebook’s decision to block authoritative news sources would further expose the company to the spread of misinformation and unverified content.
 “It’s very important that we have a diverse and well-resourced news media sector in Australia, that’s a critical part of our democracy. Now, that may not seem important to a company in Silicon Valley, but it’s very important to the Australian government and the Australian people,” Mr Fletcher said.
 “They are effectively saying to Australians if you’re looking for reliable news Facebook is not the place to look for it.”
  Labor, who voted to support the code in House of Representatives on Wednesday, said on Thursday that the government had failed to deliver a “workable code”.
 “This is not a workable code that has been landed by this government,” communications spokeswoman Michelle Rowland said.
 “It is incumbent on this government to explain where it goes from here. The reality is they have been talking the biggest game in the world over the last couple of weeks.”
 The ban appears to have come into effect for at least some users, who are now unable to post links to Australian news articles on their Facebook pages or to see past articles posted by major media companies. Easton’s blog said that not only are Australian outlets restricted from sharing content on Facebook Pages but international articles from newspapers such as  The Wall Street journal and  The New York Times cannot be viewed or reposted by Australian users.
  “This action proves again their monopoly position and unreasonable behaviour,” the spokesperson said. “But today’s statement does not mean Facebook will not have to abide by the federal government’s proposed code. Value has already been transferred and Facebook has benefited from our content for many years.”
 “We should be able to access their monopoly platform and have the right to monetise our content as a result.”
 ABC’s managing director David Anderson said the national broadcaster was affected and were discussing the change with Facebook.
 Facebook’s decision comes after multiple discussions between Treasurer Josh Frydenberg and the company’s global chief executive Mark Zuckerberg. Talks were still occurring  as recently as Sunday and Mr Frydenberg previously described them as “constructive”.
  “Mark Zuckerberg didn’t convince me to back down, if that’s what you are asking,” Mr Frydenberg said on the ABC’s  Insiders program in late January.
 But industry sources familiar with the government’s discussions with Facebook said that Mr Frydenberg was not given any meaningful notice about Facebook’s plans to follow through with its threat today.
 Mr Frydenberg said on Twitter this morning he had just spoken to Mr Zuckerberg who raised a few more issues with the government’s newly proposed laws. “We agreed to continue our conversation to try to find a path forward,” Mr Frydenberg wrote.
  News outlets that currently have a relationship with Facebook and post articles in its newsfeed were informed early on Thursday morning that their content would be affected.
  A note sent by Facebook Australia and New Zealand’s head of news partnerships Andrew Hunter, seen by  The Sydney Morning Herald and  The Age, said the tech platform was “disappointed” that it had to follow through with its threat and that it would engage the news outlets further to discuss the changes in the coming days.
  Facebook was in talks with news outlets such as Nine Entertainment Co, owner of this masthead, and News Corp Australia about payment for their content over the past two weeks.
 But those talks stalled in the past five days because of several sticking points, including explicit provisions in Facebook’s contracts that would allow it to blow up any deal if new laws were legislated. The main reason for this is that failure to comply with the code could cost Facebook fines of up to 10 per cent of their local revenue.
 Mr Easton said the government’s proposed legislation “seeks to penalise Facebook for content it didn’t take or ask for”.
  “This legislation sets a precedent where the government decides who enters into these news content agreements, and ultimately, how much the party that already receives value from the free service gets paid,” he said.
 “We will now prioritise investments to other countries, as part of our plans to invest in new licensing news programs and experiences.”
 Facebook’s stance represents a different approach to search giant Google, which had also threatened to exit Australia. Google has since struck multi-million dollar deals with major Australian publishers for the use of their content.
 Google  has agreed to pay Nine Entertainment Co, which owns  The Sydney Morning Herald and  The Age, more than $30 million in cash annually for the use of its news content. The company has also struck similar deals with Seven West Media and overnight announced a global deal with News Corp.
  Zoe Samios is a media and telecommunications reporter at The Sydney Morning Herald and The Age.
 Lisa Visentin is a federal political reporter at The Sydney Morning Herald and The Age, covering education and communications.
title: Facebook禁止在澳大利亚分享新闻
title_en: Facebook bans news sharing in Australia

note:
CNBC最近报道说，加利福尼亚州是世界第五大经济体（根据美国商务部的数据）。他们补充说，2019年该州的风险资本（VC投资资金）为656亿美元，“迄今为止，是所有州中最高的”。
 但另一篇CNBC文章援引了预测，即硅谷风险投资的份额今年将首次下降至20％以下，这是自2014年以来一直在缓慢下降的局面。最近也有新闻报道称，硅谷的“外逃”来自硅谷的技术人员-证明事实是老的事实取决于您要查看的数据集。
 我们最终会看到趋势的暗示还是个人生活的一瞥？在大流行结束后，由于租金下降和最初吸引他们的企业家生态系统的吸引，“逃离”硅谷的科技工作者会否仅仅返回？还是我们这一年的远程工作会成为未来的预兆，为技术劳动力的分布带来真正而持久的变化？
 我们是否可以目睹云带来的全社会变革？
  1月，《纽约时报》为旧金山的科技工作者描绘了一幅令人沮丧的生活画面，他们面临着长途通勤或在“恶劣”社区工作的选择。租金是天文数字。税收很高。你的邻居不喜欢你。”现在，在大流行改变的世界中，有些人“逃到”了没有所得税的佛罗里达州或得克萨斯州，或乔治亚州的负担得起的城镇，甚至哥斯达黎加和巴哈的热带海滩城镇。 “他们突然有可变动的工作和银行里的钱-这些钱会在其他地方走得更远。”
 几年前，我从旧金山搬到了佛罗里达。人们震惊了。 “但是您将如何在南佛罗里达州开展业务？”三年后，每当另一位硅谷高管前往迈阿密，我的商业生活就表现得很好。
 -§AlexisOhanian7️⃣7️⃣6️⃣（@alexisohanian）2020年12月6日 
一位思科设计师告诉《泰晤士报》，他在科罗拉多州博尔德市购买了130万美元的房屋，“我们只在视频上看到了20分钟，然后同意。与同一笔钱相比，这是一栋豪宅。”
 但是，仍然有技术工人正在进入硅谷。去年12月，“大技术”博客着重介绍了LinkedIn创建的“技术迁移”电子表格，该电子表格为数十万美国技术人员汇编了自己报告的位置数据。该电子表格将一个地区的新技术人员流入与其流出（以简单比率捕获）进行了比较，还计算了与上一年相比的变化。
 到2020年，旧金山湾区似乎停滞不前-尽管其流入与流出的技术人员的比率不是1/1，而是0.96。
 从匹兹堡和底特律地区开始，其他总共15个地区的人口损失比旧金山多，其次是波士顿，辛辛那提，芝加哥，弗吉尼亚的汉普顿路地区，大密尔沃基和纽约市附近的大都市地区。
 数据确实显示了巨大的变化。对于2019年，旧金山地区的相同比率为1.48，使该地区的同比变化为-35.29％，是电子表格上41个地区中最大的。 Big Technology博客指出：“美国仅有的两个失去技术工人的城市是纽约市和旧金山。” “旧金山以前每剩下100人增加148名工人；现在仅增长96。
 博主写道：“这些急剧下降可能只是暂时的，因为这些城市可能在大流行后反弹。”他分享了LinkedIn首席经济学家Karin Kimbrough的评论，他暗示一些居民可能稍后会回到2020年离开的小镇。 “我们认为这些举措中的很多可能是短期的。我们预计，当办公室重新开放时，技术人才将重返湾区等传统技术中心。”
   CNBC指出，从该州的总人口（不仅仅是硅谷的技术工人）来看，从2019年7月1日到2020年7月1日，加州增加了21,200人，而离开该州的人口增加了135,600人，这是有记录以来的第三大净损失。 
CNBC的一段视频报道援引人口普查数据指出：“事实上，加利福尼亚流失到其他州的人数超过了过去二十年来的大部分。”
  CNBC还注意到，9月份全州的房价中位数超过了712,000美元。加州财政部的人口统计学家沃尔特·施瓦默（Walter Schwarm）告诉他们，该州3950万人口中的一小部分可能还有其他因素。例如，加利福尼亚州人口老龄化的人口减少了，自2017年严格的移民政策生效以来，每年向该州的国际移民人数都在下降。
 SaaS公司Markodojo的创始人乔尔·约克（Joel York）认为，但不要冲销硅谷。彭博社编辑委员会的一篇评论文章指出“公司正在逃离加利福尼亚。责备坏政府，”约克在评论中反驳说：“ CA的技术行业并不围绕大型的，老式的科技公司，例如甲骨文和惠普。他们是难题的最后一部分，因为这些人正从事收购创新者的业务。”
 在后来的评论中，他补充说：“硅谷的引擎围绕着风险投资公司，企业家，大学人才，基础广泛的技术劳动力（包括大人物），以及最重要的是将它们联系在一起的网络的生态系统。 。一旦企业在快速增长后稳定下来，向德克萨斯州派遣大型高科技工作类似于外包以降低成本。德州在所有其他三个方面以及将它们联系在一起的网络上还有很长的路要走。”
 这不会让人感到惊讶，但是风险投资家是否改变城市并不重要。真正的问题是关于企业家和关键职能主管（总是其他市场的限制因素）。金钱总是在流动。
   第三个故事来自数据，这些数据显示了新公司的成立地点和总部。风险投资基金Initialized Capital的合伙人金梅·卡特勒（Kim-Mai Cutler）在研究了总部在200多家公司的投资组合中的总部位置（及其年度创始人调查的结果后，平均每年90家公司）后，发表了自己的想法。卡特勒的结论？
 她在最近的博客文章中写道：“峰湾区是在七年前的2014年。” “在2008年金融危机之后，该地区不仅价格便宜，而且拥有强大的技术人才队伍。但是到了2010年代中期，生活成本上涨的压力和压力已广为人知，在招聘方面与大技术公司竞争变得越来越困难，因此随着公司开始扩散，这种趋势开始逆转。”卡特勒写道，到2018年初，只有不到一半的公司位于北加利福尼亚州-今年，位于湾区的公司只占其投资组合的不到三分之一。 
但是她也引用了一个更有趣的发现。根据他们最近的创始人调查的结果，大流行之后，他们所投资的公司中有36％将完全处于偏远或分散经营（到2020年2月不到20％）。即使对于其余公司，“基准期望”仍将包括在家工作两到三天。 （6月，《纽约时报》报道说，Twitter，Facebook，Zillow和Shopify已经在制定“永远在家工作”的计划。）
 因此，这是Initialized调查中最有趣的结果。当被问及哪个城市对计划长期成功的初创企业最有利时，旧金山湾区仍然获得最多的选票（28.4％），是其次接近的竞争对手奥斯丁和纽约的四倍（均为6.8％） ）。
  CNBC科技记者马特·罗索夫（Matt Rosoff）（在海湾地区居住了17年）强调说，一些世界上最大的科技公司仍深深扎根于硅谷。尽管苹果，Facebook和Alphabet正在进行数十亿美元的重建，但“ Alphabet和Salesforce在湾区雇用了大约30,000名工人，仅在旧金山就建立了数十万平方英尺的办公空间” –更不用说湾了Twilio，Zoom，Airbnb，Doordash和Pinterest等地区性公司。罗索夫（Rosoff）预测，仅凭这一点就足以吸引企业家雇员，他们最终将创办自己的公司-依靠来自湾区风险投资公司的资金-还将涌现出一些名列前茅的大学毕业生，如U.C.。伯克利和斯坦福大学“拥有强大的本地网络和与科技行业的联系”。
 同样，彭博社编辑委员会也承认：“数十年来，对硅谷消亡的预测确实被证明是错误的。”
 但是，即使外逃确实发生了，仍然存在我们如何应对的剩余问题。在给《洛杉矶时报》的一封信中，一位70岁的加利福尼亚州居民就技术工人可能外流的问题发表了自己的看法。
    对于“流动”工人，日产设想了一种露营车，其特色是从行李箱缩回的小隔间。
note_en:
CNBC recently reported that California has the world’s fifth-largest economy (based on figures from the U.S. Department of Commerce). And they add that in 2019 the state saw $65.6 billion in venture capital (VC investment money), “by far the highest of any state.”
 But another CNBC article cites predictions that Silicon Valley’s share of VC investments will  drop below 20% for the first time this year — the continuation of a slow decline that’s been happening since 2014. And there’s also recently been news stories about an “exodus” of tech workers from Silicon Valley — proving the old truth that what’s happening depends on which data set you’re looking at.
 Are we ultimately seeing hints of trends or just glimpses of individual lives? Will tech workers “fleeing” Silicon Valley simply return when the pandemic is over, drawn by declining rents and the ecosystem of entrepreneurs that had attracted them in the first place? Or will our year of working remotely become a harbinger of things to come, bringing real and lasting changes to the distribution of the tech workforce?
 Could we be witnessing a society-wide transformation that’s made possible by the cloud?
  In January the New York Times  painted a glum picture of life for San Francisco’s tech workers who’d faced choices between long commutes or working in “bad” neighborhoods. “Rent was astronomical. Taxes were high. Your neighbors didn’t like you.” Now in a world changed by the pandemic, some have “fled” to states like Florida or Texas where there’s no income tax, or to affordable towns in Georgia, or even tropical beach towns in Costa Rica and Baja. “They have suddenly movable jobs and money in the bank — money that will go plenty further somewhere else.”
 Few years ago I moved from SF to Florida. People shocked. “But how will you do business from South Florida?” Three years later, my business life doing pretty well 😏 and headlines every time another Silicon Valley exec leaves for Miami.
 — §AlexisOhanian 7️⃣7️⃣6️⃣ (@alexisohanian)  December 6, 2020
  A Cisco designer told the Times he’d bought a $1.3 million home in Boulder, Colorado “that we saw only on video for 20 minutes and said yes. It’s a mansion compared to SF for the same money.”
 But there’s also still tech workers who are moving into Silicon Valley. In December  the “Big Technology” blog spotlighted  a “tech migration” spreadsheet created by LinkedIn that compiled self-reported location data for several hundred thousand U.S. tech workers. That spreadsheet compared a region’s inflow of new tech workers with its outflow (captured in a simple ratio), also calculating how much it’s changed from the previous year.
 And in 2020 the San Francisco Bay Area appears close to a stasis — though its ratio of inflowing to outflowing tech workers is not 1/1, but .96.
 A total 15 other regions lost more people than San Francisco — starting with the Pittsburgh and Detroit areas, followed by metropolitan areas around Boston, Cincinnati, Chicago, the Hampton Roads region of Virginia, greater Milwaukee, and New York City.
 The data does show dramatic changes. For 2019, the same ratio in the San Francisco area had been 1.48 — giving the region a year-over-year change of -35.29%, the largest of any of the 41 regions on the spreadsheet. “The only two cities in the country losing tech workers that previously were gaining them are New York City and San Francisco,” notes the Big Technology blog. “San Francisco was previously gaining 148 workers for every 100 that left; now it’s gaining only 96.
 “These steep drops may only be temporary, however, as these cities will likely bounce back post-pandemic,” writes the blogger, sharing comments from LinkedIn chief economist Karin Kimbrough who suggests some residents may later return to the town’s they left in 2020. “We believe a lot of these moves are likely short-term. We expect to see tech talent returning to legacy tech hubs like the Bay Area when offices reopen.”
   CNBC notes that when looking at the state’s total population — not just Silicon Valley’s tech workers — California added 21,200 people from July 1, 2019, to July 1, 2020, while 135,600 more left the state, the  third-largest net loss ever recorded.
 Citing census data, a CNBC  video report also notes that “In fact, California has lost more people to other states than it’s gained for much of the last two decades.”
  CNBC also notes that in September the median home price for the entire state was over $712,000. The small dip in the state’s population of 39.5 million people may have other factors, they’re told by Walter Schwarm, a demographer with California’s Department of Finance. For example, California’s aging population is having fewer children — and international immigration into the state has dropped every year since 2017 when stricter immigration policies went into effect.
 But don’t write off Silicon Valley, argues  Joel York, founder of SaaS company Markodojo. When an  opinion piece by Bloomberg’s editorial board argued “Companies Are Fleeing California. Blame Bad Government,” York countered in a comment that “The tech industry in CA does not revolve around big, old tech companies like Oracle and HP. They are the last piece of the puzzle because these guys are in the business of acquiring the innovators.”
 In a later comment, he added that “The engine of Silicon Valley revolves around the ecosystem of VC firms, entrepreneurs, university talent, a broad-based tech labor pool (which includes the big guys), and most importantly the networks that connect them. Sending big tech jobs to Texas is akin to outsourcing to reduce costs once a business stabilizes after rapid growth. Texas has a long way to go on all the other three fronts, and the networking that links them together.”
 This will surprise no one, but it really doesn’t matter if VCs change cities. The real question is about entrepreneurs AND key functional executives (always a limiter for other markets). Money always travels.
   A third story emerges from data showing where new companies are being started and headquartered.  Kim-Mai Cutler, a partner at the VC fund  Initialized Capital, shared her own thoughts after studying the locations of headquarters within their portfolio of over 200 companies (as well as the results of its annual founder survey, averaging 90 companies a year). Cutler’s conclusion?
 “Peak Bay Area was seven years ago in 2014,” she wrote  in a recent blog post. “Following the 2008 financial crash, the region was both substantially cheaper and had a strong corps of technical talent. But by the mid-2010s, the stresses and pressures of the rising cost of living became widely known, it became much harder to compete against Big Tech companies on recruiting and so this trend began reversing as companies began spreading out.” By the start of 2018 fewer than half their companies were in Northern California — and this year Bay Area-based companies represent less than a third of their portfolio, Cutler writes.
 But she also cites an even more intriguing find. Based on results from their  most recent founders survey, after the pandemic, 36% of their portfolio companies will be fully remote or decentralized (compared to less than 20% in February of 2020). And even for the remaining companies “baseline expectations” will still include two to three days of working from home. (In June the New York Times  reported that Twitter, Facebook, Zillow and Shopify are already developing “work from home forever” plans.)
 So here’s the most interesting result from  Initialized’s survey. When asked which city would be most beneficial for startups planning for long-term success, the San Francisco Bay Area still received the most votes (28.4%), more than four times its next nearest competitors, Austin and New York (both at 6.8%).
  CNBC Tech reporter Matt Rosoff (a Bay Area resident of 17 years) emphasizes that some of the world’s largest tech companies  still have deep roots in the Valley. While Apple, Facebook, and Alphabet are undertaking billion-dollar redevelopments, “Alphabet and Salesforce employ about 30,000 workers in the Bay Area, and have built out hundreds of thousands of square feet of office space in San Francisco alone” —  not to mention Bay Area-based companies like Twilio, Zoom, Airbnb, Doordash and Pinterest. Rosoff predicts that alone is enough to attract entrepreneurial employees who will eventually start companies of their own — flush with capital from Bay Area-based VCs — and there’ll also be a steady influx of graduates from top schools like U.C. Berkeley and Stanford “with strong local networks and connections to the tech industry.”
 Similarly, Bloomberg’s editorial board conceded that “predictions of Silicon Valley’s demise have proved reliably wrong for decades.”
 But even if an exodus does materialize, there’s still the remaining question of how we’ll react. In a letter  to the Los Angeles Times, one 70-year California resident shared his own opinion about a possible exodus of tech workers.
    For “mobile” workers, Nissan imagines a camper van featuring  a cubicle that retracts from the trunk.
title: 科技工作者正在“逃离”硅谷吗？ 数据显示如下
title_en: Are tech workers 'fleeing' Silicon Valley? Here's what the data shows

note:
在Ars，我们涵盖了Valve禁止Steam游戏开发人员进行的一切活动，从色情内容和免费的超暴力行为到定义不明确的＆＃34;拖钓＆＃34;。但是我们之前从未见过这样的情况：开发人员因为其（非侵权）名称而被Steam开除。这就是Emoji Evolution开发人员Very Positive发生的事情，该公司周六在Twitter上表示，其开发人员帐户已被禁止进行审查操作。但是，与Steam使用者评论操纵的其他突出示例不同，“非常肯定”并没有采取任何措施来过分歪曲玩家为其游戏发布的评论。
  取而代之的是，Very Positive利用了Steam商店用户界面的一个变体。该界面以与该游戏总体用户评论摘要的书面摘要相同的字体，颜色和一般区域显示游戏的开发者和发行者名称（例如，压倒性的肯定， 34;＆＃34;＆＃34;＆＃34;＆＃34;＆＃34;＆＃34;等。）因此，用户很难一眼就分辨出＆＃34;非常积极＆＃34;开发者名称不是Emoji Evolution实际用户评论的准确摘要（根据屏幕截图，其范围从``混合''到``最积极''）。西蒙·卡利斯（Simon Carless）是最早注意到这种骗术的人之一，他在2月8日的GameDiscoverCo时事通讯中对此进行了报道。在接下来的几天中，非常积极的存在和行动的消息在游戏新闻网站和社交媒体中流传开来。到2月12日，开发人员已被禁止使用Steam。
    我知道评论会对客户的决定产生重大影响，非常肯定（由化名麦克带走）背后的编码员告诉Vice。 ＆＃34;我注意到发布者/开发者的名字确实位于评论附近，并且具有相同的颜色，因此我决定将其用于我的目的。迈克说，Steam用户在看到熟悉的单词时可以得出有关信息的结论，而不必花费大量时间阅读所有单词。
 迈克似乎正在大步走下禁令，在Twitter上宣传有关传奇的模因和笑话。甚至在Steam禁令发布之前，Very Positive帐户就发推文说，老实说，开发人员和发行人的名字是整个Emoji Evolution项目中最好的事情，＆＃34;承认游戏本身的廉价基础。
 我做了一个非常糟糕的游戏-这是我唯一有罪的事情，＆＃34;非常正面周三发表推文。 ＆＃34;如果Steam上不允许制作糟糕的游戏，为什么他们还没有暂停CDPR帐户？ （Z！）
 最后，Very Positive试图在Steam的拜占庭式商店系统中利用很小的注意力毛刺，但失败的部分原因是太多的人给予了它注意力。不过，展望未来，我们不禁要想，在Valve的角色上进行UI更改可能比管制单个开发人员的姓名看起来像假的更有效（且易于实现）。查看摘要。或者，就像Twitter用户DoctorWyrm所说的那样（在Very Positive转推的推文中），也许Valve应该修复他们易于利用的审查系统，而不是仅仅禁止开发者。
title: Steam开发人员因“非常积极”的审查技巧而被禁止
title_en: Steam developer gets banned for “Very Positive” review trickery

多年来，台式机操作系统市场份额的每份年度报告都具有相同的两个竞争者：微软的Windows排名第一，苹果的macOS排名第二。但是在2020年，Chrome操作系统成为第二流行的操作系统，而苹果则跌至第三。
  这是根据市场数据公司IDC的数字以及出版物GeekWire关于IDC数据的报告得出的。 Chrome操作系统在各个季度之前已经短暂地通过了macOS，但是2020年是苹果操作系统排名第三的第一年。尽管macOS排名第三，但将其视为Google直接击败苹果的示例可能并不准确。相反，Chrome OS可能主要是在低端市场上将销售和市场份额从Windows上拉了下来。 Mac市场份额实际上从2019年的6.7％增长到2020年的7.5％。
    同时，Chrome操作系统从2019年的6.4％激增至2020年的10.8％。Windows则从85.4％下降至80.5％。
 趋势似乎在这里受到Google的青睐，但2020年离正常年份还很远。上个月，IDC关于PC销量的报告显示，传统PC（台式机，笔记本电脑，工作站）的销量连续几年保持稳定增长。即便如此，IDC仍表示，销售增长在很大程度上是由教育市场内外的Chromebook扩张所驱动。
 由于许多社区的学生不得不在家中上课，而父母也必须在远程上班，因此PC销量在这一年跃升了，其中Chrome OS占了很大一部分。但是整个市场整体增长，而不仅仅是Chrome操作系统。 IDC还指出，游戏PC是增长的主要推动力，加上Mac在各种用例方面的表现尤其强劲。
 由于世界某些国家可能在今年或明年晚些时候发现一种新型的大流行后或晚大流行常态，因此新的销售数字将更清楚地表明未来的发展方向，而不仅仅是2020年的发展方向。但是至少在教育领域，Chrome操作系统的未来看起来相当光明。
note_en:
For ages now, every annual report on desktop operating system market share has had the same top two contenders: Microsoft's Windows in a commanding lead at number one, and Apple's macOS in distant second place. But in 2020, Chrome OS became the second-most popular OS, and Apple fell to third.
  That's according to numbers by market data firm IDC and a  report on IDC's data by publication GeekWire. Chrome OS had passed macOS briefly in individual quarters before, but 2020 was the first full year when Apple's OS took third place. Despite the fact that macOS landed in third, viewing this as an example of Google beating out Apple directly might not be accurate. Rather, it's likely that Chrome OS has been primarily pulling sales and market share away from Windows at the low end of the market. Mac market share actually grew from 6.7 percent in 2019 to 7.5 percent in 2020.
    Meanwhile, Chrome OS skyrocketed from 6.4 percent in 2019 to 10.8 percent in 2020. Windows fell from 85.4 percent to 80.5 percent.
 The trend looks to be in Google's favor here, but 2020 was far from a normal year. Last month, IDC's report on PC sales showed the first year of  consistent growth of traditional PC (desktop, laptop, workstation) sales in years. Even then, IDC indicated that the increase in sales was driven in large part by the expansion of Chromebooks both within and outside of the education market.
 As students in many communities have had to attend class virtually from home, and their parents have had to do work remotely too, PC sales jumped during the year, and Chrome OS was a big part of that. But the entire market grew overall, not just Chrome OS. IDC also noted that gaming PCs were a big driver of growth, plus a particularly strong year for the Mac for various use cases.
 As some of the world may find a new kind of post- or late-pandemic normalcy later this year or next year, new sales figures will give a clearer indication of where things will go in the future, not just how they went in 2020. But at least in the education space, the future of Chrome OS looks fairly bright.
title: 世界第二受欢迎的台式机操作系统不再是macOS
title_en: The world’s second-most popular desktop operating system isn’t macOS anymore

 或者，到目前为止，您已经清醒了，飞机的轮子已经安全地滑到停机坪上了。您了解到，在商业航班上发生任何形式的事故的机率都比苗条的机率低，而且您可能永远不必使用此信息。

For more than a century, Popular Mechanics  has provided life-saving advice for outlasting storms, surviving outdoors, and preparing for disaster. Find out how to survive anything  right here.
      You have a late night and an early flight. Not long after takeoff, you drift to sleep. Suddenly, you're wide awake. There's cold air rushing everywhere, and sound. Intense, horrible sound.  Where am I?, you think.  Where's the plane?
  Things are bad. But now's the time to focus on the good news. (Yes, it goes beyond surviving the destruction of your aircraft.) Although gravity is against you, another force is working in your favor: time. Believe it or not, you're better off up here than if you'd slipped from the balcony of your high-rise hotel room after one too many drinks last night.
 Or at least you will be. Oxygen is scarce at these heights. By now, hypoxia is starting to set in. You'll be unconscious soon, and you'll cannonball at least a mile before waking up again. When that happens, remember what you are about to read. The ground, after all, is your next destination.
 Granted, the odds of surviving a 6-mile plummet are extra­ordinarily slim, but at this point you've got nothing to lose by understanding your situation. There are two ways to fall out of a plane. The first is to free-fall, or drop from the sky with absolutely no protection or means of slowing your descent. The second is to become a wreckage rider, a term coined by Massachusetts-based amateur historian Jim Hamilton, who developed the Free Fall Research Page—an online database of nearly every imaginable human plummet.
  That classification means you have the advantage of being attached to a chunk of the plane. In 1972, Serbian flight attendant Vesna Vulovic was traveling in a DC-9 over Czechoslovakia when it blew up. She fell 33,000 feet, wedged between her seat, a catering trolley, a section of aircraft, and the body of another crew member, landing on—then sliding down—a snowy incline before coming to a stop, severely injured but alive.
 Surviving a plunge surrounded by a semi-protective cocoon of debris is more common than surviving a pure free-fall, including perennial Ripley's Believe It or Not superstar Alan Magee—blown from his B-17 on a 1943 mission over France. The New Jersey airman fell 20,000 feet and crashed into a train station; he was subsequently captured by German troops, who were astonished at his survival.
 Whether you're attached to crumpled fuselage or just plain falling, the concept you'll be most interested in is  terminal velocity. As gravity pulls you toward earth, you go faster. But like any moving object, you create drag—more as your speed increases. When downward force equals upward resistance, acceleration stops. You max out.
 Depending on your size and weight, and factors such as air density, your speed at that moment will be about 120 mph—and you'll get there after a surprisingly brief bit of falling: just 1,500 feet, about the same height as Chicago's Willis Tower. Equal speed means you hit the ground with equal force. The difference is the clock. Body meets Windy City sidewalk in 12 seconds.
 From an airplane's cruising altitude, you'll have almost enough time to read this entire article.
     By now, you've descended into breathable air. You sputter into consciousness. At this altitude, you've got roughly 2 minutes until impact. Your plan is simple. You will enter a Zen state and decide to live. You will understand, as Hamilton notes, "that it isn't the fall that kills you—it's the landing."
  But at what? Magee's landing on the stone floor of that French train station was softened by the skylight he crashed through a moment earlier. Glass hurts, but it gives. So does grass. Haystacks and bushes have cushioned surprised-to-be-alive free-fallers. Trees aren't bad, though they tend to skewer. Snow? Absolutely. Swamps? With their mucky, plant-covered surface, even more awesome.
  Hamilton documents one case of a sky diver who, upon total parachute failure, was saved by bouncing off high-tension wires. Contrary to popular belief, water is an awful choice. Like concrete, liquid doesn't compress. Hitting the ocean is essentially the same as colliding with a sidewalk, Hamilton explains, except that pavement (perhaps unfortunately) won't "open up and swallow your shattered body."
 With a target in mind, the next consideration is body position. To slow your descent, emulate a sky diver. Spread your arms and legs, present your chest to the ground, and arch your back and head upward. This adds friction and helps you maneuver. But don't relax. This is not your landing pose.
 The question of how to achieve ground contact remains, regrettably, given your predicament, a subject of debate. A 1942 study in the journal  War Medicine noted "distribution and compensation of pressure play large parts in the defeat of injury." Recommendation: wide-body impact. But a 1963 report by the Federal Aviation Agency argued that shifting into the classic sky diver's landing stance—feet together, heels up, flexed knees and hips—best increases survivability. The same study noted that training in wrestling and acrobatics would help people survive falls. Martial arts were deemed especially useful for hard-surface impacts: "A 'black belt' expert can reportedly crack solid wood with a single blow," the authors wrote, speculating that such skills might be transferable.
 The ultimate learn-by-doing experience might be a lesson from Japanese parachutist Yasuhiro Kubo, who holds the world record in the activity's banzai category. The sky diver tosses his chute from the plane and then jumps out after it, waiting as long as possible to retrieve it, put it on and pull the ripcord. In 2000, Kubo—starting from 9,842 feet—fell for 50 seconds before recovering his gear. A safer way to practice your technique would be at one of the wind-tunnel simulators found at about a dozen U.S. theme parks and malls.
 But neither will help with the toughest part: sticking the landing. For that you might consider—though it's not exactly advisable—a leap off the world's highest bridge, France's Millau Viaduct; its platform towers 891 feet over mostly spongy farmland.
 Water landings—if you must—require quick decision-making. Studies of bridge-jump survivors indicate that a feet-first, knife-like entry (aka "the pencil") best optimizes your odds of resurfacing. The famed cliff divers of Acapulco, however, tend to assume a head-down position, with the fingers of each hand locked together, arms outstretched, protecting the head. Whichever you choose, first assume the free-fall position for as long as you can. Then, if a feet-first entry is inevitable, the most important piece of advice, for reasons both unmentionable and easily understood, is to  clench your butt.
 No matter the surface, definitely don't land on your head. In a 1977 "Study of Impact Tolerance Through Free-Fall Investigations," researchers at the Highway Safety Research Institute found that the major cause of death in falls—they examined drops from buildings, bridges and the occasional elevator shaft (oops!)—was cranial contact. If you have to arrive top-down, sacrifice your good looks and land on your face, rather than the back or top of your head. You might also consider flying with a pair of goggles in your pocket, Hamilton says, since you're likely to get watery eyes—impairing accuracy—on the way down.
     Given your starting altitude, you'll be just about ready to hit the ground as you reach this section of instruction (based on the average adult reading speed of 250 words per minute). The basics have been covered, so feel free to concentrate on the task at hand. But if you're so inclined, here's some supplemental information—though be warned that none of it will help you much at this point.
 Statistically speaking, it's best to be a flight crew member, a child, or traveling in a military aircraft. Over the past four decades, there have been at least a dozen commercial airline crashes with just one survivor. Of those documented, four of the survivors were crew, like the flight attendant Vulovic, and seven were passengers under the age of 18. That includes Mohammed el-Fateh Osman, a 2-year-old wreckage rider who lived through the crash of a Boeing jet in Sudan in 2003.
 Crew survival may be related to better restraint systems, but there's no consensus on why children seem to pull through falls more often. The Federal Aviation Agency study notes that kids, especially those under the age of 4, have more flexible skeletons, more relaxed muscle tonus, and a higher proportion of subcutaneous fat, which helps protect internal organs. Smaller people—whose heads are lower than the seat backs in front of them—are better shielded from debris in a plane that's coming apart. Lower body weight reduces terminal velocity, plus reduced surface area decreases the chance of impalement upon landing.
     The ground. Like a Shaolin master, you are at peace and prepared.  Impact. You're alive. What next? If you're lucky, you might find that your injuries are minor, stand up and smoke a celebratory cigarette, as British tail gunner Nicholas Alkemade did in 1944 after landing in snowy bushes following an 18,000-foot plummet. More likely, you'll have tough work ahead.
 Follow the example of Juliane Koepcke. On Christmas Eve 1971, the Lockheed Electra she was traveling in exploded over the Amazon. The next morning, the 17-year-old German awoke on the jungle floor, strapped into her seat, surrounded by fallen holiday gifts. Injured and alone, she pushed the death of her mother, who'd been seated next to her on the plane, out of her mind. Instead, she remembered advice from her father, a biologist: To find civilization when lost in the jungle, follow water. Koepcke waded from tiny streams to larger ones. She passed crocodiles and poked the mud in front of her with a stick to scare away stingrays. She had lost one shoe in the fall and was wearing a ripped miniskirt. Her only food was a bag of candy, and she had nothing but dark, dirty water to drink. She ignored her broken collarbone and her wounds, infested with maggots.
  On the tenth day, she rested on the bank of the Shebonya River. When she stood up again, she saw a canoe tethered to the shoreline. It took her hours to climb the embankment to a hut, where, the next day, a group of lumberjacks found her. The incident was seen as a miracle in Peru, and free-fall statistics seem to support those arguing for divine intervention: According to the Geneva-based Aircraft Crashes Record Office, 118,934 people have died in 15,463 plane crashes between 1940 and 2008.
 Even when you add failed-chute sky divers, Hamilton's tally of confirmed or plausible lived-to-tell-about-it incidents is only 157, with 42 occurring at heights over 10,000 feet.
 But Koepcke never saw survival as a matter of fate. She can still recall the first moments of her fall from the plane, as she spun through the air in her seat. That wasn't under her control, but what happened when she regained consciousness was. "I had been able to make the correct decision—to leave the scene of the crash," she says now. And because of experience at her parents' biological research station, she says, "I did not feel fear. I knew how to move in the forest and the river, in which I had to swim with dangerous animals like caimans and piranhas."
 Or, by now, you're wide awake, and the aircraft's wheels have touched safely down on the tarmac. You understand the odds of any kind of accident on a commercial flight are slimmer than slim and that you will likely never have to use this information.
title: 如何跌落35,000英尺并生存
title_en: How To Fall 35,000 Feet and Survive

工人呼吁麦当劳，汉堡王和温迪提高工资，因为国会准备辩论联邦最低工资
    15个城市的快餐店员工将于周二举行“黑人历史月”罢工，要求麦当劳，汉堡王和温迪的饭店连锁店每小时支付15美元。
 采取这一行动之际，国会正准备就最低工资从目前的7.25美元提高至15美元进行辩论，这是自2009年以来的首次联邦加薪。
  家庭护理和养老院工人将加入亚特兰大，查尔斯顿，芝加哥，底特律，休斯敦和圣路易斯等城市的工人，以支持15美元的最低工资和加入工会的权利。
 乔·拜登（Joe Biden）承诺在竞选期间提高最低工资，但最近表示，提高工资可能无法纳入他试图推动国会通过的$ 1.9tn冠状病毒救助计划。拜登表示，这一增长对于他缩小种族经济不平等的承诺至关重要。
 进步的参议员包括伯尼·桑德斯（Bernie Sanders），他是工会支持的争取15美元运动的长期支持者。
 众议院议长南希·佩洛西（Nancy Pelosi）周四证实，工资上涨将成为众议院法案的一部分，但在参议院中，民主党仅占少数，因此面临着更艰难的斗争。民主党希望通过一项被称为“预算和解”的程序来通过该法案，该程序只需要参议院的简单多数即可。 
“这是黑人历史月，我们有机会赢得每小时至少15美元的生活工资，并使数百万家庭摆脱贫困，从而创造自己的历史，”来自南卡罗来纳州查尔斯顿的麦当劳工人Taiwanana Milligan说。
 Milligan补充说：“几十年来，麦当劳从像我这样的工人的背上赚了数十亿美元，向我们支付了饥饿的工资。我今天很惊讶，因为我每小时至少需要15美元才能生存，而且我知道做出改变的唯一方法就是站起来，大声疾呼并要求改变。”
 八年抗争15美元运动近年来取得了重大进展，包括加利福尼亚，佛罗里达和纽约在内的各州投票决定将其最低工资提高到每小时15美元。
 虽然民意测验显示多数美国人赞成增加选票，但大多数共和党政客，一些民主党人和游说团体仍持反对意见。
 国会预算办公室本周发布的一份报告估计，有2700万美国人将从加薪中受益，并使90万人摆脱贫困。但报告还警告说，加薪将导致140万个工作岗位，并大大增加该国的债务。其他经济学家对这一发现提出了质疑，左倾的经济政策研究所称其为“错误和不当夸大”。
 麦当劳（McDonald）来自得克萨斯州休斯顿的20岁工人格洛丽亚·马胡卡（Gloria Machuca）说，她目前的时薪为每小时9.50美元。她说：“这还远远不够。” “十五美元意味着我可以花更多的时间陪伴孩子，而不用担心挨饿，付账。”
 她说，在冠状病毒大流行期间，通过法案更为重要。她说：“这非常令人担忧。” “考虑我如何支付医生的账单，生病时必须工作非常令人紧张。”她说，仅仅缺一天的工作对于她的财务状况将是一个“重大挫折”。 
“我对拜登总统的信息是，传递这一点非常重要，这不仅对我而且对所有正在苦苦挣扎的其他人都将产生很大的影响。”
note_en:
Workers call for McDonald’s, Burger King and Wendy’s to increase pay as Congress prepares to debate federal minimum wage
    Fast-food workers in 15 cities will hold a  Black History Month strike on Tuesday to demand that the McDonald’s, Burger King and Wendy’s restaurant chains pay them $15 an hour.
 The action comes as Congress prepares to debate a federal rise in the minimum wage to $15 from its current rate of $7.25, the first federal raise since 2009.
  Workers in cities including Atlanta, Charleston, Chicago, Detroit, Houston and St Louis will be joined by home care and nursing home workers in support of a $15 minimum wage and the right to join a union.
 Joe Biden pledged to increase the minimum wage during his election campaign but has recently suggested the increase may not make it into the $1.9tn coronavirus relief package he is trying to push through Congress. Biden has said the increase is central to his pledge to narrow racial economic inequality.
 Progressive senators including Bernie Sanders, a longtime supporter of the union-backed Fight for $15 movement, which has campaigned for the rise, and others are working to ensure the federal raise remains in the package.
 On Thursday Nancy Pelosi, the House speaker, confirmed the wage rise will be part of the House bill but it faces a tougher fight in the Senate where Democrats have only a slight majority. Democrats hope to pass the bill through a process known as  “budget reconciliation” which would require only a simple majority in the Senate.
 “This Black History Month, we have a chance to make our own history by winning a living wage of at least $15 an hour and lifting millions of families out of poverty,” said Taiwanna Milligan, a McDonald’s worker from Charleston, South Carolina.
 Milligan added: “For decades, McDonald’s has made billions in profit off the backs of workers like me, paying us starvation wages. I’m striking today because I need at least $15 an hour to survive and because I know the only way to make change is to stand up, speak out and demand it.”
 The eight-year Fight for $15 movement has made significant progress in recent years with states including California, Florida and New York voting to increase their minimum wages to $15 an hour.
 While  polling shows the increase is favored by the majority of Americans, opposition remains from most Republican politicians, some Democrats and lobby groups.
 A  Congressional Budget Office report published this week calculated 27 million Americans would benefit from a raise and 900,000 people would be lifted out of poverty. But the report also cautioned the raise would cost 1.4m jobs and add significantly to the nation’s debt. The findings have been disputed by other economists with the left leaning Economics Policy Institute calling them  “wrong and inappropriately inflated”.
 Gloria Machuca, a 20-year McDonald’s worker from Houston, Texas, said she was currently making $9.50 an hour. “It is not nearly enough,” she said. “Fifteen dollars would mean I could spend more time with my children, not worry about going hungry, paying bills.”
 She said passing the bill was even more important during the coronavirus pandemic. “It’s been very worrying,” she said. “It’s very stressful thinking about how I can pay doctor’s bills, having to work when I could get sick.” Missing just one day of work would be a “major setback” for her financially she said.
 “My message to President Biden is it’s very important to pass this, it would make a big difference not just for me but for all the others that are struggling.”
title: 美国快餐店工人举行“黑人历史月”罢工，要求每小时支付15美元
title_en: US fast food workers hold Black History Month strikes to demand $15 an hour
